# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder:14b)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

# Use an appropriate base image, here we use a Ubuntu-based image as an example
FROM ubuntu:latest

# Install dependencies and packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    nginx \
    postgresql \
    redis-server \
    curl

# Configure and start PostgreSQL
COPY init-db.sh /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/
RUN chmod +x /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-db.sh

# Configure and start Redis
RUN sed -i 's/supervised no/supervised systemd/g' /etc/redis/redis.conf

# Copy web server configuration (e.g., Nginx)
COPY nginx.conf /etc/nginx/nginx.conf

# Copy API server application code
COPY api-server /api-server

# Expose necessary ports
EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Start all services in the background
CMD service postgresql start && \
    redis-server /etc/redis/redis.conf & \
    nginx -g "daemon off;" & \
    cd /api-server && npm install && node app.js
